macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Job Market Rebounds Strongly in March with 178,000 New Jobs
items.summary: The U.S. added 178,000 jobs in March, significantly exceeding economist expectations, with the unemployment rate declining to 4.3%. The rebound reversed February's unexpected job losses, though underlying wage growth remains pressured by inflation.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge Above $100 as Iran Conflict Disrupts Supply
items.summary: WTI crude oil prices have neared $100 per barrel following airstrikes on Iran, triggering a four-week stock market decline and pushing U.S. gasoline prices above $4 per gallon for the first time since 2022.

items.title: Fed Officials Warn of Energy-Driven Inflation Risks
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and other officials warned that rising energy prices from the Iran conflict could spread inflation across multiple economic sectors, with Goldman Sachs revising inflation forecasts higher for 2026.

items.title: 30-Year Mortgage Rates Rise to 6.46%
items.summary: The average rate on a 30-year fixed mortgage increased to 6.46% this week, up from 6.38% the previous week, reflecting broader market pressures from energy-driven inflation concerns.

items.title: Airlines Raise Baggage Fees Amid Fuel Cost Surge
items.summary: United Airlines and JetBlue announced baggage fee increases of $9-$10, citing surging jet fuel prices linked to the Iran conflict and global oil supply disruptions.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: ONG Leads Crypto Gainers with 38.39% Surge in 24 Hours
items.summary: Ontology Gas (ONG) topped the 24-hour gainers with a 38.39% price increase to $0.0911 on $32.86M volume, followed by YB up 28.71% to $0.15 on $59.66M volume. Other notable gainers include IKA (+19.90%), PTB (+19.34%), and EDGE (+18.87%).

items.title: $156 Million in Crypto Futures Liquidations Over Past 24 Hours
items.summary: Crypto derivatives markets saw $156M in liquidations, led by ETH ($47.06M) and BTC ($46.07M), followed by SOL ($11.48M) and XRP ($3.91M). This highlights ongoing market volatility amid recent price movements.

items.title: Bitmine Acquires 40,000 ETH Worth $82 Million
items.summary: Bitmine Immersion Technologies bought approximately 40,000 ETH (~$82M) via multiple transfers from FalconX wallets, increasing its Ethereum holdings. The purchase occurred as ETH traded around $2,049, signaling institutional interest.

items.title: Bitcoin Stabilizes Near $67K Amid Muted Markets
items.summary: Bitcoin traded around $66,871-$67K with a 0.34% gain, while Ethereum dipped 0.18% to $2,050. Markets remain volatile with rising liquidations and mixed altcoin performances.

items.title: High Trading Volumes for Solana and Other Altcoins
items.summary: Solana (SOL) saw high volume at $3.23B with 1.19% gain to $80.53, while EDGE surged 50.88% on $174.63M volume and ARIA gained 15.51% on $75.46M. JuChain (JU) recorded massive $2.27B volume.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Tohoku University Demonstrates Living Brain Cells for Machine Learning
items.summary: Researchers at Tohoku University and Future University Hakodate trained living rat cortical neurons in a reservoir computing framework to perform supervised temporal pattern learning tasks, producing complex time-series signals comparable to artificial systems. The breakthrough uses microfluidic devices for precise network control and suggests biological neural networks as alternatives to traditional ML models.

items.title: Microsoft Announces $13B Investment in Japan for AI and Cyber Defense
items.summary: Microsoft plans to invest $13 billion in Japan to expand AI and cyber defense capabilities, causing Sakura Internet's stock to jump 20%. The move highlights growing enterprise AI infrastructure investments in Asia.

items.title: OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show TBPN in First Media Purchase
items.summary: OpenAI bought Silicon Valley's TBPN, a popular tech-focused talk show, rooted in decade-long ties with its founder, aiming to drive AI conversations. This marks OpenAI's entry into media amid its expanding 'side quests'.
items.source: Silicon Republic / Ars Technica / The Next Web

items.title: Take-Two Lays Off AI Team Including Head of Artificial Intelligence
items.summary: Take-Two Interactive laid off an unspecified portion of its AI team, including the Head of AI, as part of ongoing industry restructuring. This follows similar cuts at other firms amid AI focus shifts.

items.title: Meta Cuts 200 Bay Area Jobs to Invest in AI Infrastructure
items.summary: Meta is eliminating approximately 200 positions in the San Francisco Bay Area while redirecting resources to bolster AI infrastructure. Oracle is also reportedly laying off staff for similar AI-focused restructuring.

items.title: AI Talent Costs Surge with Higher Base Salaries for Top Experts
items.summary: Startups are offering higher base salaries over equity to compete for elite AI talent, driving up costs amid intense demand. This trend reflects the booming AI job market.

items.title: IBM and Arm Partner on Dual-Architecture Systems for AI Workloads
items.summary: IBM and Arm are collaborating to develop dual-architecture enterprise systems optimized for AI workloads, enhancing efficiency in data processing and machine learning tasks.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: State of Decay 3 Alpha Playtest Announced
items.summary: Undead Labs has announced an alpha playtest for State of Decay 3, allowing select players to sign up via the official website for early access. The game will feature improved four-player co-op, enhanced base-building, resource strategies, and intense combat, with no release date revealed yet.

items.title: Crimson Desert Receives Major Patch Update
items.summary: The latest Crimson Desert patch significantly increases camp stash inventory capacity and enables fast travel to Abyss Nexuses, while fixing a broken traversal ability, improving PS5 Pro PSSR, 4K upscaling on Xbox Series X, and addressing stability issues across platforms.

items.title: Rumors Swirl of Star Fox Return on Nintendo Switch
items.summary: Leaker Nate the Hate claims a new Star Fox game is coming to Nintendo Switch this year, fueled by Fox McCloud's inclusion in the Super Mario Galaxy movie and recent Nintendo leaks, though the leaker's track record is mixed.

items.title: Last of Us Online Director Learned of Cancellation Just 24 Hours Prior
items.summary: The director of the canceled Last of Us Online multiplayer project revealed they were informed of the cancellation only 24 hours before it was public.

items.title: Resident Evil Celebrates 30 Years with Enduring Fan Obsession
items.summary: BBC discusses Resident Evil's 30th anniversary, highlighting its role in spawning the survival horror genre, with insights from Resident Evil Requiem's producer on maintaining freshness amid zombie tales and intricate gameplay.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: MIT Engineers Develop Lightning Strike Prediction Tool for Aircraft and Wind Turbines
items.summary: MIT engineers have designed a simulation tool to predict lightning strikes on modern aircraft, which could also reduce damage to wind turbines. The model enhances safety for aviation and renewable energy infrastructure.

items.title: US Scientists Create Superconducting Qubit Lasting 15x Longer Than Standard
items.summary: Researchers have built a superconducting qubit that maintains coherence 15 times longer than industry standards by redesigning it with a two-pronged approach. This advances quantum computing stability.

items.title: NASA and JAXA Test X-59 Supersonic Model in Mach 1.4 Wind Tunnel
items.summary: NASA and JAXA conducted wind tunnel tests on a hand-sized X-59 model at Mach 1.4 speeds to gather data on quiet supersonic flight. The tests support development of low-boom supersonic aircraft.

items.title: New Optical Chip Enables Ultra-Fast Computing and Data Processing
items.summary: Scientists developed a silicon photonics-based optical chip for next-generation ultra-fast computing and data processing. It promises significant speed improvements in tech hardware.

items.title: Harvard Textile Reduces Wind Drag by Up to 20% for Better Performance
items.summary: Harvard researchers created a new textile that performs better in high wind speeds by cutting drag up to 20%. It has potential in sports gear and engineering applications.
